Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

In this case, while the patch looks trivial, I think it is wrong. Also, because it is not testing the DNE code that this affects, it may well be missing the failure during non-DNE testing...

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

Any defect in the code should probably be fixed in a separate patch. That said, the only danger would be "thread A unlock, thread B lock, thread B change + sync, MDS crash" before thread A sync?  Even so, it isn't harmful if thread B gets a write in there as long as the MDS doesn't crash in the window between thread A write+sync, and even so wouldn't the thread B sync also result in the thread A changes being flushed to disk?

It doesn't seem like a particular priority to fix.

Patch Set 1:

well, the whole point of this logic about sync/decref logic is to prevent other clients to modify these objects before the change is committed (CoS), right? but if sync=1, then it's broken because "commit" part happens after the locks are released.

Patch Set 1:

Sure, but if the current code is broken with gcc8 that could be fixed easily, and the issue you present may be much more complex and slow to fix.

commit.subject: LU-12069 mdt: add missing argument to enable compilation
commit.message:
LU-12069 mdt: add missing argument to enable compilation

with gcc8 which is very strict about missing arguments.

Change-Id: I08cd4a876c4cb49e15ada7458a637c98ea4d83c0
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
